steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
At an intersection point, both equations have the same $y$-value. Since one equation says $y=76$, substitute $76$ into the other equation.
$$76=x^2-5$$

steps.title: Solve for possible inputs
steps.content:
Add $5$ to both sides and take square roots.
$$x^2=81$$
$$x=9\text{ or }x=-9$$
Only one of these values is listed.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing the two equations shows intersections with $x$-coordinates $9$ and $-9$. The listed possible value is $-9$. The answer is B.
